category_title: Students with SEBD who respond to peer provocation with reactive aggression and 'payback' behaviors
scenario_title: Reactive physical aggression following peer verbal provocation
minimal_context: The student is participating in a group assignment with peers at their desks in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please sit at your desks with your groups and start working on your assignments.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't want to sit here. They keep calling me 'fat pig' and 'glasses boy'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try to ignore it and focus on your assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: He's saying really mean things about me and my father. And he just kicked me! I know that you think that I'm bad, so you'll just go for their side.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please lower your voice and stay in your seat.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Throws a chair) They were bullying me, so I'm getting payback! I'm very, very angry and I'm about to punch somebody!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please sit at your desks with your groups and start working on your assignments.

STUDENT: I don't want to sit here. They keep calling me 'fat pig' and 'glasses boy'.

TUTOR: Just try to ignore it and focus on your assignment.

STUDENT: He's saying really mean things about me and my father. And he just kicked me! I know that you think that I'm bad, so you'll just go for their side.

TUTOR: Please lower your voice and stay in your seat.

STUDENT: (Throws a chair) They were bullying me, so I'm getting payback! I'm very, very angry and I'm about to punch somebody!

task.content: Participating in social interactions and activities with peers during unstructured time or in the classroom

context.environment: Mainstream classroom or residential school setting
context.social_setting: Peers present, interacting with the student
context.time_context: Unstructured time (recess/breaks) or general classroom time

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When peers were bullying the student, calling them names ('fat pig', 'glasses boy', 'monkey', 'Chinese boy'), saying mean things about their father, or hurting them
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Started throwing desks and chairs around, kicking, punching, and expressing extreme anger
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Felt socially isolated, rejected, sad, lonely, and very, very angry; perceived engagement in bullying as a legitimate response (self-defence or fair payback)
related_quotes: They were bullying me, calling me names, hurting me so I started throwing desks and chairs around.
They called me “fat pig ” and “glasses boy ” cos I used to wear glasses and they called me a monkey and then they call me Chinese boy. Cos I had a dad that looks like he ’s from China.
They were bullying me, saying really mean things about me and my father. And then I started to learn how to express my anger, hurt others. I would kick, punch, yeah.
I got very, very angry. I was about to punch somebody.

behavior_patterns.condition: When a peer physically hurts the student (e.g., kicked them) and teachers ignore the initial provocation
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Overreacts, starts going off to the teachers, and tries to kick the peers
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Feels the teachers think they are bad, believes teachers will just go for the other kids' side, feels it's unfair
related_quotes: If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, they (the teachers) wouldn ’t do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them.
I know that the teachers think that I ’m bad, so they ’ll just go for their side, so I don ’t bother to say anything.
